- CSS properties inside `@media` blocks getting indented
- JavaScript function bodies getting proper indentation
- HTML/SVG attributes getting aligned

message.content:
New thing.

In Safari only, v26, the nav-item-stroke is not showing up. I can see it in the markup, it's rendering. I see computed styles that seem right, but it's not visible. Even if I remove the `opacity: 0` prop from the at rest state. Can you have a look to see what might be happening

content.thinking: Let me look at the nav-item-stroke component and its styles to understand what's going on.

content.text: Let me look at the nav-item-stroke component and its styles.

content.content:
Found 4 files
src/css/tylergaw.css
src/_components/page-footer.webc
src/_components/page-header.webc
src/_components/nav-item-stroke.webc

file.content:
<script webc:type="render" webc:is="template">
  module.exports = function () {
    const seed = parseInt(this.seed) || 1;
    let s = seed | 0;
    const rand = () => {
      s = (s * 1103515245 + 12345) & 0x7fffffff;
      return s / 0x7fffffff;
    };

    const randY = () => (1 + rand() * 8).toFixed(1);

    const generatePath = () => {
      const pts = [`M0 ${randY()}`];
      for (let i = 1; i <= 4; i++) {
        const x = (i / 5) * 40 + (rand() - 0.5) * 6;
        pts.push(`L${x.toFixed(1)} ${randY()}`);
      }
      pts.push(`L40 ${randY()}`);
      return pts.join(" ");
    };

    const d = generatePath();
    const values = [d, generatePath(), generatePath(), generatePath()].join(
      ";",
    );
    const dur = (0.4 + rand() * 0.2).toFixed(2) + "s";

    return `<svg class="nav-item-stroke" viewBox="0 0 40 10" aria-hidden="true" preserveAspectRatio="none"><path d="${d}" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.4"><animate attributeName="d" calcMode="discrete" repeatCount="indefinite" dur="${dur}" values="${values}" /></path></svg>`;
  };
</script>

content.thinking:
The issue is likely the SVG filter `url(#nav-item-warp)`. Safari has known issues with SVG filters referenced by fragment ID, especially when:

1. The SVG containing the filter definition has `display: none` or `hidden` attribute
2. The filter is referenced from CSS on elements outside the SVG

Looking at the filter SVG in layout-main.webc (line 148-171), it has both `hidden` and `style="display: none"` attributes. Safari is notoriously picky about this - when the SVG is hidden with `display: none`, Safari may not render filters defined within it.
The SVG also has `width="0"` and `height="0"`, which combined with the hidden attributes likely prevents Safari from resolving the filter reference entirely. Since the user reports the element renders but stays invisible even without the opacity, the filter failure is probably rendering the element as blank rather than applying the intended effect.

Safari has a known issue where `filter: url(#id)` fails when the defining SVG is hidden, so the solution is to either use `visibility: hidden` instead of `display: none`, switch to `position: absolute` with zero dimensions and `overflow: hidden`, or move to a CSS-only filter approach. Let me examine the SVG attributes more closely to determine which fix applies best.

content.text:
I think I see the problem. The SVG filter `#nav-item-warp` is defined inside an SVG element that has both `hidden` and `style="display: none"` (layout-main.webc:150-151). Safari has a known issue where it won't resolve `filter: url(#id)` references when the defining SVG is hidden with `display: none`. It effectively makes the filtered element invisible — which matches what you're seeing.

Let me check the full SVG wrapper.

      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
      width="0"
      height="0"
      aria-hidden="true"
      hidden
      style="display: none"
    >

          <feTurbulence
            type="turbulence"
            baseFrequency="0.06"
            numOctaves="3"
            seed="4"
            result="turbulence"
          />
          <feDisplacementMap
            in="SourceGraphic"
            in2="turbulence"
            scale="4"
            xChannelSelector="R"
            yChannelSelector="G"
          />
        </filter>
      </defs>
    </svg>

    <page-header></page-header>

content.text:
That confirms it. The SVG on line 145-171 uses `hidden` and `style="display: none"` to hide the filter definitions. Safari (especially v26/WebKit) won't resolve `filter: url(#nav-item-warp)` from a `display: none` SVG, so the filtered `.nav-item-stroke` element becomes invisible.

The fix is to hide the SVG visually without using `display: none` or `hidden`. Replace those with a positional off-screen approach that keeps the SVG in the rendering tree:

- Remove `hidden` and `style="display: none"`
- Add `style="position: absolute; width: 0; height: 0; overflow: hidden"`

Want me to make that change?

file.content:
# tylergaw.com Project Guide

## General Approach

- **Don't just start writing code**: Always understand the requirements and confirm first
- **Ask follow-up questions**: Make sure you fully understand what's needed before proceeding
- **Get confirmation before writing large amounts of code**: Check the approach before implementing
- **Don't make assumptions**: If something is unclear, ask rather than guessing
- **Don't prompt me**: When you complete a task, don't ask; "if I want to next?"

### Code

- CSS properties should be alphabetized
- DO NOT run builds after making changes. Only run builds if told to

## Writing Blog Posts

### Copyediting Guidelines

When helping with blog post copyediting:

- **Focus on**: typos, grammar, sentence structure
- **Do NOT provide story editing advice** unless explicitly requested
- **Keep edits atomic**: work at sentence or paragraph level, one at a time
- **Avoid giving large amounts of feedback at once** - pause after the first issue and let the author step through them

### Writing Style

- **Conversational tone**: Writing should be close to how the author speaks
- **Not every sentence needs to be complete**: Fragment sentences are acceptable when they sound natural
- **Short, punchy sentences are preferred**: Average 10-12 words per sentence
- **Look for opportunities to**:
  - Shorten sentences
  - Omit unnecessary words
  - Turn comma-separated clauses into separate sentences
  - Break up compound sentences

### Typography

- **Always use smart quotes (curly quotes) in prose**: " " ' '
- **Never use straight quotes in prose**: " '
- **Reference**: https://smartquotesforsmartpeople.com/
- Smart quotes are for prose; straight quotes are only for code or when discussing punctuation

### Tone Considerations

- **Watch for defensive tone**: Avoid language that feels like justifying or defending decisions
- **Matter-of-fact is better**: State things directly without over-explaining
- **Trust the reader**: Don't preemptively defend against potential criticism

### Reading Level

- Target: 8th-9th grade reading level (accessible for personal blog)
- Use simpler words when possible without losing the author's voice
- Avoid overly formal language

<script webc:bucket="components">
  /*
    NOTE: We do an initial check/set of the scheme in layout-main.webc to avoid
    an initial flash if the user has chosen dark scheme.
  */

  function getScheme() {
    return localStorage.getItem(COLOR_SCHEME_STORAGE_KEY) || "light";
  }

  function applyScheme(scheme) {
    document.documentElement.style.colorScheme = scheme;
    document.querySelectorAll("scheme-chooser button").forEach((btn) => {
      btn
        .querySelector(".icon-light")
        .classList.toggle("hidden", scheme !== "dark");
      btn
        .querySelector(".icon-dark")
        .classList.toggle("hidden", scheme === "dark");
    });
  }

  function toggleScheme(e) {
    e.preventDefault();
    const next = getScheme() === "light" ? "dark" : "light";
    localStorage.setItem(COLOR_SCHEME_STORAGE_KEY, next);
    applyScheme(next);
  }

  // Apply stored preference on load
  applyScheme(getScheme());

  document.querySelectorAll("scheme-chooser button").forEach((btn) => {
    btn.addEventListener("click", toggleScheme);
  });
</script>

<style webc:scoped>
  button {
    --size: 1.5rem;
    align-items: center;
    appearance: none;

    &:hover,
    &:focus-visible {

      );

      .button-stroke {
        opacity: 1;
      }
    }

    svg {
      grid-area: 1 / 1;
    }

    .icon {
      margin: 0 auto;
      height: 1rem;
      width: 1rem;
    }

    .button-stroke {
      opacity: 0;
      filter: url(#nav-item-warp);
    }

    .hidden {
      opacity: 0;
    }
  }
</style>

content.text: Now let me look at the nav-item-stroke to understand the animation pattern.

file.content:
<script webc:type="render" webc:is="template">
  module.exports = function () {
    const seed = parseInt(this.seed) || 1;
    let s = seed | 0;
    const rand = () => {
      s = (s * 1103515245 + 12345) & 0x7fffffff;
      return s / 0x7fffffff;
    };

    const randY = () => (1 + rand() * 8).toFixed(1);

    const generatePath = () => {
      const pts = [`M0 ${randY()}`];
      for (let i = 1; i <= 4; i++) {
        const x = (i / 5) * 40 + (rand() - 0.5) * 6;
        pts.push(`L${x.toFixed(1)} ${randY()}`);
      }
      pts.push(`L40 ${randY()}`);
      return pts.join(" ");
    };

    const d = generatePath();
    const values = [d, generatePath(), generatePath(), generatePath()].join(
      ";",
    );
    const dur = (0.4 + rand() * 0.2).toFixed(2) + "s";

    return `<svg class="nav-item-stroke" viewBox="0 0 40 10" aria-hidden="true" preserveAspectRatio="none"><path d="${d}" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.4"><animate attributeName="d" calcMode="discrete" repeatCount="indefinite" dur="${dur}" values="${values}" /></path></svg>`;
  };
</script>

content.text: So the nav-item-stroke uses a squiggly animated path with an `feTurbulence`/`feDisplacementMap` filter for extra warp. For the circle button-stroke, I'll add an inline SVG filter with turbulence displacement and animate the seed so it has that jittery/squiggly feel on hover — similar vibe but self-contained in scheme-chooser.

input.new_string:
<svg
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
    width="24"
    height="24"
    viewBox="-1 -1 24 24"
  >
    <defs>
      <filter id="button-stroke-warp">
        <feTurbulence
          type="turbulence"
          baseFrequency="0.06"
          numOctaves="3"
          seed="2"
          result="turbulence"
        >
          <animate
            attributeName="seed"
            values="2;8;3;10;5;12;7;2"
            dur="0.4s"
            calcMode="discrete"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
          />
        </feTurbulence>
        <feDisplacementMap
          in="SourceGraphic"
          in2="turbulence"
          scale="4"
          xChannelSelector="R"
          yChannelSelector="G"
        />
      </filter>
    </defs>
    <circle
      cx="11"
      cy="11"
      r="10"
      stroke="currentColor"
      stroke-width="1.2"
      fill="none"
      class="button-stroke"
    />
  </svg>

content.text:
Done. Here's what changed:

- Added a self-contained `<filter id="button-stroke-warp">` inside the button-stroke SVG with `feTurbulence` + `feDisplacementMap` (same approach as `nav-item-warp`)
- The filter animates its `seed` attribute across 8 values at `0.4s` with `calcMode="discrete"` — gives it that jittery squigglevision effect matching the nav strokes
- Updated the CSS to reference `#button-stroke-warp` instead of `#nav-item-warp`
